final_phrase: WHEN TIME FORGETS THE ROAD, FOLLOW THE ADDRESS THAT REMEMBERS.
start_message:
THE LOST COORDINATE

My dear investigators,

Permit me to introduce myself with as much composure as my circumstances allow.

My name is Edmund Grey. I am a member of the Meridian Society of London, and I have arrived in Moscow from the year 1896.

I was sent here to investigate a dangerous irregularity in the historical record. An English address in old Moscow — a place that once held the memory of trade, diplomacy and trust between two distant worlds — has begun to fade from the map of time.

Not from stone.
Not from paper.
From memory.

During my passage, my chronometer was struck by a force we call the Chronophage: the eater of time. It does not destroy cities. It does something far more discreet. It persuades people to stop noticing them.

Now I am trapped in your century.

My Meridian Key is broken, and its fragments have hidden themselves in Moscow’s visible evidence: gates, facades, monuments, street names and old addresses.

I must return to 1896 before the final meeting of the Meridian Society. If I fail, London will close the Moscow file forever, and the English trace in this city will become no more than an old wall with an unread sign.

I ask for your assistance.

Do not hurry.
Do not merely photograph.
Do not accept the city as background.

Observe with care, answer with precision, and help me restore the address that remembers.

Type START if you accept the case.
safety_message:
Before we begin, a gentleman must insist upon certain rules.

First, stay together. No respectable investigation is conducted by abandoning one’s companions.

Second, cross streets only where it is proper and safe to do so. Time travel is dangerous enough without reckless traffic decisions.

Third, do not enter closed courtyards, private spaces or restricted buildings.

Fourth, speak English as much as possible. Accuracy is admirable, but courage in speech is even more useful.

Finally, if the city confuses you, consult your teacher before consulting panic.

Type I AGREE to proceed.
finish_message:
The case is complete.

WHEN TIME FORGETS THE ROAD, FOLLOW THE ADDRESS THAT REMEMBERS.

The Meridian Key was never a pocket instrument. It was an address — a fixed point where London’s memory survived inside Moscow.

You restored the route.
You repaired the memory.
You gave time its road back.

London is no longer lost.
Moscow is no longer a labyrinth.
And Edmund Grey may, at last, return to 1896.

Final team task:
Discuss or record a 60-second answer in English.

Why was the Meridian Key a place rather than an object?

Use at least three of these words:
address, memory, road, time, London, Moscow, evidence.

locations.title: Stop 1: A Proper Departure
locations.message:
STOP 1: A PROPER DEPARTURE

My dear investigators,

No respectable journey begins with a dramatic gesture. It begins with a point from which one may depart.

A careless traveller stands here, takes a photograph and moves on. A gentleman, however, understands that before one may be lost, one must first have departed from somewhere.

This bronze marker beneath your feet is not merely decorative. It establishes the formal beginning of distance. For a man whose century has gone missing, such a beginning is not a luxury. It is a necessity.

Look down and consider what a journey becomes at the moment it leaves its starting point.
locations.question: In formal British English, what word can describe the act of leaving a starting point at the beginning of a journey?
hints: Think of a railway station board: arrivals and ______.
The word begins with D and means leaving for a journey.
locations.wrong_reply:
Not quite, I’m afraid.

You have understood the beginning, but I require the more formal travelling word: the opposite of arrival.
locations.success_reply:
Excellent.

DEPARTURE is restored.

Every journey requires a proper departure, and my lost century has finally acquired a point from which to be approached.

Fragment 1 unlocked:

WHEN

A gentleman may now proceed.
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss while walking:

Why do stories often begin at gates, doors or borders?
locations.next_directions: Next stop: Resurrection Gate. Walk a short distance to the red gate between Manezhnaya Square and Red Square. Stand where you can see the passage clearly. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Stop 2: The Threshold
locations.address: Between Manezhnaya Square and Red Square
locations.message:
STOP 2: THE THRESHOLD

My dear companions,

A gate is never just an opening in a wall. It separates before from after, outside from inside, ordinary time from ceremonial time.

The modern traveller rushes through gates without ceremony. A gentleman pauses, looks up, and asks what kind of entrance he is being permitted to cross.

Stand where you can see the passage clearly. I require not the number of towers, but the idea this structure performs.

The Chronophage dislikes gates. Gates remind the city that crossing requires attention.
locations.question: What do we call the boundary or entrance that separates one space from another and must be crossed before the journey continues?
hints: Think about what a gate does, not only what it looks like.
It is the point where “before” becomes “after”.
locations.wrong_reply:
A hasty answer, but haste is the enemy of time.

Look again at the gate as a formal boundary, not merely as architecture.
locations.success_reply:
Very good.

The THRESHOLD is restored.

I can feel the passage stabilising. The Chronophage dislikes gates because they make people notice that they are entering another space.

Fragment 2 unlocked:

TIME
locations.speaking_task: Use the phrase “a threshold between…” and describe this gate in one sentence.
locations.next_directions: Next stop: Kazan Cathedral. Walk into Red Square and approach the small cathedral near the corner with Nikolskaya Street. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Stop 3: The Echo of Another City
locations.message:
STOP 3: THE ECHO OF ANOTHER CITY

My dear investigators,

You are now standing near a building that performs a curious trick. It belongs to Moscow, stands in Moscow, is seen by thousands in Moscow — and yet its name carries the echo of another city.

This is most useful to us.

The Meridian Key was designed to recognise places that contain more than one layer of memory. A name may be a doorway. A city may hide inside a city.

Read the name of the cathedral. Notice how one city sounds inside another.
locations.question: When one place preserves the memory or sound of another place inside its name, what kind of repeated trace is it?
hints: The answer is not the city name itself. It is what that city name becomes inside Moscow.
The word describes a sound or memory that returns.
locations.wrong_reply:
Not quite.

You have found a religious building, yes, but I asked what the other city becomes inside its name. Read the title once more, then think more carefully.
locations.success_reply:
Splendid.

The ECHO is restored.

A city hidden inside a name — that is precisely the kind of memory the Chronophage tries to flatten.

Fragment 3 unlocked:

FORGETS
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss:

Can a name carry memory even when people no longer think about its meaning?
locations.next_directions: Next stop: GUM. Cross carefully toward the long historic facade on Red Square. If possible, enter briefly and look at the covered passage. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Stop 4: The Covered Passage
locations.message:
STOP 4: THE COVERED PASSAGE

My dear companions,

A gentleman must distinguish between a shop and a system of space.

This building is not only a collection of stores. It is a covered urban passage: a street brought indoors, disciplined by glass, light and repetition. People walk through it as if through a city within the city.

Time changes governments. Time changes habits. Time even changes what people consider elegant. But certain forms of public space remain legible.

If you enter or look carefully from the entrance, observe the long covered passage and the rhythm of the galleries.
locations.question: What architectural word describes a covered passage with shops or galleries along it?
hints: Think of a covered street inside a building.
The word is often used for historical shopping passages in European cities.
locations.wrong_reply:
A respectable attempt, but the evidence has not yet been properly read.

Do not name the building. Name the type of covered urban space it contains.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

The ARCADE is restored.

The facade has held its name, and the passage has held its form. Names fixed in public space are small acts of resistance against forgetting.

Fragment 4 unlocked:

THE
locations.speaking_task: Describe the space using the words “public”, “survive” and “attention”.
locations.next_directions: Next stop: Monument to Minin and Pozharsky. Walk toward the monument in front of St Basil’s Cathedral. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Stop 5: The Bronze Witnesses
locations.address: Red Square, in front of St Basil’s Cathedral
locations.message:
STOP 5: THE BRONZE WITNESSES

My dear investigators,

Bronze is a most reliable witness. It does not gossip, exaggerate or check its phone. It simply remains.

Before you stand two figures held in a single historical gesture. Yet a monument has two voices: the visible gesture above and the written authority below.

A careless observer looks only at the figures. A careful one reads the lower support, because names, dates and dedications often stand there.

Approach respectfully and look at the part that supports the figures.
locations.question: What do we call the base of a monument where names or inscriptions are usually placed?
hints: Do not focus only on the bronze figures. Look at what supports them.
It is the lower part of a statue or monument.
locations.wrong_reply:
Not quite, I’m afraid.

Let us not be hurried into an ungentlemanly conclusion. Look below the figures, where the monument usually carries its written evidence.
locations.success_reply:
Excellent work.

The PEDESTAL is restored.

The bronze witness has spoken. The Chronophage is weaker near monuments because people keep asking who is being remembered.

Fragment 5 unlocked:

ROAD
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss:

Why might a seated or still figure still look powerful?
locations.next_directions: Next stop: Varvarka Street. Leave Red Square toward the beginning of Varvarka near the Church of St Barbara. Look for a street plaque. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Stop 6: The Street’s Memory
locations.location_name: Varvarka Street / Church of St Barbara
locations.message:
STOP 6: THE STREET’S MEMORY

My dear companions,

We now leave the grand theatrical space and enter a street with a more private memory.

A square announces itself. A street, however, must be followed. That is why I prefer streets. They demand time, direction and a willingness not to know everything at once.

Look for the small object fixed to a wall that tells travellers where they are. It is easy to ignore, yet without it a route becomes rumour.

The Chronophage begins its work with such small signs. If people stop reading them, streets lose their memory.
locations.question: What do we call the flat sign attached to a wall that carries a street name or official information?
hints: Look for the blue street-name object on a nearby wall.
It is flat, fixed to a surface, and carries written information.
locations.wrong_reply:
Not the building, not the church, not the district.

I need the word for the small written object that keeps the street’s name in public view.
locations.success_reply:
Precisely.

The PLAQUE is restored.

Varvarka is not merely a street. It is a corridor of centuries, and the route is becoming clearer.

Fragment 6 unlocked:

FOLLOW
locations.speaking_task: While walking, describe the street atmosphere in two sentences: one factual, one emotional.
locations.next_directions: Next stop: Romanov Boyars’ Chambers. Continue along Varvarka toward house 10 and find the museum sign. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Stop 7: The House of Deep Time
locations.message:
STOP 7: THE HOUSE OF DEEP TIME

My dear investigators,

Here the city becomes older and more private.

This place does not present itself as a palace in the grand European sense. It belongs to a different vocabulary of old Moscow: stone rooms, vaulted interiors, family life, rank and memory.

This place is not merely old. It is genealogical. It preserves a name that became more than a family name. In certain centuries, a surname can become a political weather system.

Read the museum name carefully. The key word I require is not the family name, but the type of historical space preserved here.
locations.question: What word in the museum name describes old historical rooms or formal private spaces?
hints: Do not send the family name. Look for the word that describes the old rooms themselves.
locations.wrong_reply:
Not quite.

You are close to the right century, but the chronometer needs the word for the historical rooms, not the family name.
locations.success_reply:
Very good.

CHAMBERS is restored.

Family memory, state memory, city memory — all these layers make time harder to erase.

Fragment 7 unlocked:

THE
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss:

How can a family name become part of a country’s history?
locations.next_directions: Next stop: Church of St Maximus the Blessed. Walk back toward Varvarka 4 and find the smaller church sign near the final area. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Stop 8: The Quiet Clue
locations.location_name: Church of St Maximus the Blessed
locations.message:
STOP 8: THE QUIET CLUE

My dear companions,

We are now close enough to the final address that imprecision would be most unfortunate.

This small place asks for quieter observation. Read the sign and identify not simply a name, but the role that makes the name important in religious memory.

A gentleman does not collect words blindly. He distinguishes a person from a title, and a title from a function.

Precision matters. The Chronophage does not usually attack grand monuments first. It begins with small signs no one bothers to read.
locations.question: According to the church sign, what religious role does the remembered person have?
hints: Look at the full title of the church. It tells you how the person is remembered.
The answer is not the person’s name. It is the religious status or role.
locations.wrong_reply:
Not quite.

You have given me either too much or the wrong part of the sign. I require the religious role, not simply a name.
locations.success_reply:
Excellent.

SAINT is restored.

Your precision does you credit.

The chronometer is now pointing not to an object, but to an address. I had feared this possibility. The Meridian Key may be larger than I imagined.

Fragment 8 unlocked:

ADDRESS
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss:

Why might a small or quiet place be important in a mystery?
locations.next_directions: Final stop: Old English Court. Walk a few steps toward Varvarka 4A. Look at the museum and its name. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Stop 9: The Last English Address
locations.message:
STOP 9: THE LAST ENGLISH ADDRESS

My dear investigators,

At last, we have reached the point to which the chronometer has been conducting us.

I must confess my error. I believed the Meridian Key would be a portable object: a brass key, a seal, a watch mechanism, something a gentleman might keep safely in a waistcoat pocket.

But Moscow has corrected me.

The key is not a possession.
It is a location with a name.
A place where England once had a fixed presence in Moscow.
A point to which letters, visitors, merchants and memory could be directed.

In England, a gentleman’s card is incomplete without one essential piece of information: where he may be found.

Look at this place not as a building only, but as that piece of information made of stone.
locations.question: What word describes the formal location where a person, institution or memory may be found?
hints: A gentleman may print it on his calling card.
It tells others where someone or something may be found.
locations.wrong_reply:
Not quite.

You are looking at the building, but I need the word for what the building becomes in the logic of this case: the formal place where memory may be found.
locations.success_reply:
You have found it.

ADDRESS.

The Meridian Key was never a pocket instrument. It was an address — the last English coordinate I could still trust in Moscow.

The Chronophage could not erase this place while someone was still able to read it.

You have read it.

Therefore, you have restored it.

Fragment 9 unlocked:

THAT REMEMBERS

Final message restored:

WHEN TIME FORGETS THE ROAD, FOLLOW THE ADDRESS THAT REMEMBERS.

London is no longer lost.
Moscow is no longer a labyrinth.
And I, at last, may return to 1896.
locations.speaking_task:
Final team task:

Discuss or record a 60-second answer in English:
Why was the Meridian Key a place rather than an object?
Use at least three of these words: address, memory, road, time, London, Moscow, evidence.
locations.next_directions: This is the final stop. The case is complete.
